skill.name: langgraph
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through building LangGraph agents with explicit graphs, typed state, reducers, routing, persistence, and human review flows.
skill.summary: Build structured LangGraph agents with state, routing, persistence, tools, and review workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review confirmed one high-risk issue: a LangGraph calculator tool example uses eval() on unsanitized tool input. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown formatting and benign text, with no evidence of prompt injection or data exfiltration.

high_findings.title: Unsafe Dynamic Code Evaluation in Calculator Example
high_findings.description: The calculator tool returns str(eval(expression)) on tool input. In a LangGraph agent, tool input can be influenced by a user or model, so copying this example can lead to arbitrary Python code execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The eval call is direct and uses the expression parameter without validation. The surrounding context defines it as a callable agent tool, which confirms the execution risk.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Analyzer Matches for Backticks and Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: The external command findings match Markdown code fences or inline backticks, not Ruby or shell execution. The weak cryptography findings match ordinary prose or code structure, and no weak cryptographic algorithm is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows Markdown formatting and benign LangGraph examples rather than command execution or cryptography. No code imports or calls weak crypto functions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsafe Dynamic Code Evaluation in Calculator Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The calculator tool returns str(eval(expression)) on tool input. In a LangGraph agent, tool input can be influenced by a user or model, so copying this example can lead to arbitrary Python code execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The eval call is direct and uses the expression parameter without validation. The surrounding context defines it as a callable agent tool, which confirms the execution risk.

content.value_statement: LangGraph agents can become hard to reason about when state, routing, and tools are implicit. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design explicit graph workflows with clearer state and review points.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to define LangGraph state with TypedDict and reducers.
Shows how to build StateGraph nodes, edges, and conditional routes.
Covers ReAct-style agents that call tools through LangGraph nodes.
Describes persistence, checkpointers, and resumable agent execution.
Highlights human-in-the-loop review patterns for agent workflows.
Lists anti-patterns such as infinite loops and oversized state objects.
limitations: Examples focus on Python LangGraph rather than TypeScript workflows.
It does not install dependencies or verify package versions.
The included calculator example uses unsafe eval() and should be replaced.
It provides guidance, not a complete production agent template.

use_cases.title: Design a Support Agent Graph
use_cases.description: Create a support workflow with classification, tool use, escalation, and final response routing.

use_cases.title: Review Agent State Design
use_cases.description: Check whether graph state uses reducers, private fields, and clear update boundaries.

use_cases.title: Plan Human Review Steps
use_cases.description: Add approval checkpoints before sensitive actions such as refunds, writes, or external tool calls.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Graph
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple LangGraph workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a basic LangGraph agent for this task: [task]. Include the state fields, nodes, edges, and stopping condition.

prompt_templates.title: Add Conditional Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this linear agent flow into a LangGraph workflow with conditional routing: [flow]. Explain each route and when it reaches END.

prompt_templates.title: Review State and Reducers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this LangGraph state design: [state description]. Identify reducer needs, overwrite risks, and fields that should be private.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Controls
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for persistence and human review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design production controls for this LangGraph agent: [agent description]. Include checkpointing, human approval points, retry limits, and observability needs.

output_examples.input: Design a research assistant with search, synthesis, and final report steps.
output: A state outline with messages, findings, sources, current step, and completion status.
A node list for research, synthesis, quality review, and final writing.
A routing plan that loops only while more research is needed and stops at a clear final state.

output_examples.input: Review my agent loop because it sometimes runs forever.
output: A diagnosis of missing exit conditions and weak iteration tracking.
Recommendations for a maximum step counter, task completion flag, and timeout policy.
A safer routing strategy that returns END when the task is complete or limits are reached.

output_examples.input: Add human approval before my agent sends an external request.
output: A workflow plan with a review node before the external action.
State fields for approval status, reviewer notes, and pending action details.
Routing rules for approve, reject, revise, and cancel outcomes.

best_practices: Define minimal state fields and add reducers only where values must accumulate.
Give every cycle a clear exit condition, iteration limit, or timeout.
Use checkpointing and human review before irreversible or sensitive actions.
anti_patterns: Do not use eval() or similar dynamic execution in agent tools.
Do not build loops without an END route or maximum iteration policy.
Do not place unrelated data in one large shared state object.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants design and review LangGraph workflows for stateful agent applications.

faq.question: Does it generate complete applications?
faq.answer: It provides architecture guidance and examples, but it is not a full application scaffold.

faq.question: Which language does it focus on?
faq.answer: The guidance focuses on Python LangGraph examples and related LangChain patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with human-in-the-loop workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers approval checkpoints, review routing, and state fields for reviewer decisions.

faq.question: Is the included calculator example safe?
faq.answer: No. The eval() pattern should be replaced with a safe parser or fixed-operation calculator.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
